 {"id":"gt-9za0","title":"Mol Mall: Molecule marketplace distribution","description":"Design the Mol Mall distribution mechanism for sharing molecules.

## Core Insight

Mol Mall is just a registry of molecules.jsonl fragments. No special format needed.

## Distribution Model

```
Mol Mall (registry)
    │
    │ bd mol search "security"
    ▼
Molecule catalog (searchable index)
    │
    │ bd mol install mol-security-scan
    ▼
Download molecules.jsonl fragment
    │
    │ Append to ~/.beads/molecules.jsonl
    ▼
Available locally: bd mol list
```

## Registry Format

Simple JSON index served from HTTPS:

```json
{
  "molecules": [
    {
      "id": "mol-security-scan",
      "title": "Security Scan",
      "description": "OWASP Top 10 vulnerability checker",
      "labels": ["plugin", "code-review", "tier:sonnet"],
      "author": "anthropic",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "url": "https://mol-mall.anthropic.com/molecules/mol-security-scan.jsonl"
    }
  ]
}
```

## CLI Commands

```bash
bd mol search <query>           # Search registry
bd mol info <id>                # Show molecule details
bd mol install <id>             # Download and install
bd mol uninstall <id>           # Remove from catalog
bd mol update                   # Update all installed molecules
```

## Installation Locations

```
~/.beads/molecules.jsonl        # User-level (default install location)
~/gt/.beads/molecules.jsonl     # Town-level (shared across rigs)
.beads/molecules.jsonl          # Project-level (team-specific)
```

## Version Management

Molecules can have versions via labels: `version:1.0.0`
Install specific version: `bd mol install mol-security-scan@1.0.0`

## Auth (Future)

Private registries could require auth token.
Enterprise Mol Malls for internal distribution.

## Related

- gt-u818: Plugin System (plugins ARE molecules)

 {"id":"gt-ay1r","title":"gt molecule current: Show what agent should be working on","description":"Query what an agent identity is supposed to be working on via breadcrumb trail.

## Command
```bash
gt molecule current <identity>
gt molecule current gastown/furiosa
gt molecule current deacon
```

## Logic
1. Find handoff bead for identity (pinned bead titled "<role> Handoff")
2. Parse attachment field → molecule ID
3. If no attachment → "naked" (no active molecule)
4. If attached → load molecule, find current step:
   - bd ready --parent=<mol-id> → next unblocked step
   - Or first in_progress step

## Output
```
Identity: gastown/furiosa
Handoff:  gt-8v2 (Furiosa Handoff)
Molecule: gt-mol-abc (mol-polecat-work)
Progress: 3/8 steps complete
Current:  gt-mol-abc.4 - verify-tests
```

Or if naked:
```
Identity: gastown/angharad
Handoff:  gt-9x1 (Angharad Handoff)
Molecule: (none attached)
Status:   naked - awaiting work assignment
```

## Use Cases
- Mayor checking what polecats are doing
- Witness verifying polecat progress
- Debug: "why isnt this polecat working?"
- Deacon patrol: track all agent states","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"gastown/dementus","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:34:01.430109-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:43:41.533695-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:43:41.533695-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented gt molecule current command"}
 {"id":"gt-az41","title":"mol-ready-work variants: safe vs aggressive","description":"## Summary

Create two variants of mol-ready-work:

### mol-ready-work-safe (default)
- PRs: review and comment, but require human approval for merge
- Issues: triage only, don't auto-close
- Beads: implement but create PR instead of pushing to main

### mol-ready-work-aggressive  
- PRs: can approve and merge directly
- Issues: can close duplicates/invalid
- Beads: can push directly to main

## Implementation
Could be a single molecule with a `{{mode}}` variable, or two separate protos.

## Parent
Part of gt-tnca (mol-ready-work epic)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:38.703698-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:38.703698-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-az41","depends_on_id":"gt-tnca","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:56.387233-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
